name: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Communication with a Potential Hostile Host (X-force Categorization)
notes:
This Building Block defines communication with a potential hostile host, categorized by X-force.

Note: X-force categorization is available for free of use. 

See the blog https://ibm.biz/BdPEXv for details.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Communication with a Potential Hostile Host (X-force Categorization) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System

when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.General Audit Event, System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success, Audit.Command Execution Attempt, Application.DNS In Progress
when the event matches URL Host (custom) is not N/A

when the event matches XFORCE_URL_CATEGORY("URL Host") in ('Anonymization Services','Malware', 'Botnet Command and Control Server', 'Spam URLs', 'Cryptocurrency Mining', 'Bots', 'Phishing URLs') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY BB:BehaviorDefinition: Communication with a Potential Hostile Host (X-force Categorization) on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System

AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Audit.General Audit Event, System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success, Audit.Command Execution Attempt, Application.DNS In Progress
AND when the event matches URL Host (custom) is not N/A

AND when the event matches XFORCE_URL_CATEGORY("URL Host") in ('Anonymization Services','Malware', 'Botnet Command and Control Server', 'Spam URLs', 'Cryptocurrency Mining', 'Bots', 'Phishing URLs') AQL filter query
